description: Applicants are admitted based on grades and supplementary information. Preference is given to applicants with the strongest overall qualifications. Minimum averages vary from year to year.

title: Undergraduate Admission FAQs - Overview
content: This page provides answers to common undergraduate admission questions and guides applicants to online resources. The page is updated regularly as new questions arise. Applicants may email admission@queensu.ca if their question is not listed.

title: Undergraduate Admission FAQs - Guidance Counsellors
content: Guidance Counsellors seeking admission information should refer to the Guidance Counsellor Resource page.

title: Undergraduate Admission FAQs - Viewbook
content: Applicants can download the Queen’s University Viewbook (PDF, 8 MB).

title: Admission Update - Concurrent Education (French)
content: Queen’s is accepting direct entry applications to the Concurrent Education (French) program, which dual-enrolls students in the Bachelor of Education and a Bachelor of Arts (Honours) in French. The program prepares students to become French as a Second Language teachers at the Primary/Junior or Intermediate/Senior level. Experience in Extended French or French Immersion is recommended.

title: Admission Update - Test Optional Policy
content: Queen’s University has permanently adopted a test optional admissions model. Applicants who studied in the United States are no longer required to submit SAT or ACT scores, but may submit them if they feel the scores support their application.

title: Admission Update - Science Program Course Changes
content: For Science programs, Advanced Functions 4U is no longer required. Two additional academic courses are now required.

title: Admission Update - Life Science Program Course Changes
content: For Life Science programs, Biology 4U and Chemistry 4U are required. Advanced Functions 4U is no longer required, and two additional academic courses are now required.

title: Admission Update - Health Science Math Requirement
content: Data Management 4U is no longer accepted as a math prerequisite for Health Science programs.

title: Admission Update - Provincial Curriculum Changes
content: Similar admission requirement changes have been made to other provincial curriculums. Applicants should review the admission requirements page for details.

title: Admission FAQ - Application Opening Date
content: Applications for first-year programs open in early October. Applicants should review the How to Apply page for details.

title: Admission FAQ - Applicant Groups
content: Applicants are classified as Group A or Group B applicants. OUAC provides tools to help applicants determine their classification.

title: Admission FAQ - Additional Admission Pathways
content: Applicants may be eligible for additional pathways including the First Generation Admission Pathway and the Indigenous Admission Pathway.

title: Admission FAQ - Application Limits and Offers
content: Queen’s does not make alternate offers of admission. Applicants must apply to each program individually, up to a maximum of three programs. Applicants may hold more than one offer but may accept only one by the acceptance deadline.

title: Admission FAQ - Admission Requirements Tool
content: Applicants should use the admission requirements tool to determine program-specific admission requirements.

title: Admission FAQ - Rolling Admissions
content: Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. Conditional offers may be made using available grades, including Grade 11 marks if Grade 12 marks are not yet available, provided students are registered in required Grade 12 courses.

title: Admission FAQ - Course Registration Information
content: Applicants must submit all course information regardless of semester model. If courses are unavailable or scheduling conflicts occur, students should seek guidance from their school counsellor.

title: Admission FAQ - Ontario Applicants
content: Ontario applicants must ensure their school submits all registered and projected course enrollments for the entire school year through OUAC.

title: Admission FAQ - Non-Ontario Applicants
content: Applicants outside Ontario must have their high school send completed Grade 11 coursework and registered and projected Grade 12 enrollments. Transcripts may be sent electronically to admission@queensu.ca.

title: Admission FAQ - BC and Yukon Applicants
content: BC and Yukon applicants must enter their Personal Education Number (PEN) on OUAC to ensure automatic transmission of grades.

title: Admission FAQ - CEGEP Applicants
content: CEGEP applicants must correctly enter their CEGEP Code on OUAC to ensure automatic grade transmission.

title: Admission FAQ - Other Applicants
content: Applicants not covered by other categories must have their high school submit completed Grade 11 coursework and registered and projected Grade 12 enrollments. Transcripts may be sent electronically to admdocs@queensu.ca.

title: Admission FAQ - English Language Testing
content: If an English language test is required, it will appear on the applicant’s To-Do List.

title: Admission FAQ - To-Do List and Supplementary Requirements
content: Grades are one component of admission decisions. Each applicant has a unique To-Do List in SOLUS outlining required materials and deadlines. Supplementary requirements are reviewed and updated regularly.

title: Admission FAQ - Official and Unofficial Documents
content: Applicants are expected to submit official documents when possible. Unofficial documents may be considered in exceptional circumstances. Applicants should review the unofficial documents guidelines.

title: Admission FAQ - Next Steps After Applying
content: Applicants should visit the Queen’s U Bound page for information on next steps, events, and applicant resources.

title: Admission FAQ - Submitting Documents
content: Information on submitting official documents is available on the Submit Documents page. Transcripts sent directly by students are considered unofficial.

title: International Student FAQ - English Language Requirements
content: Accepted English language proficiency tests and score requirements are listed on the English language requirements page.

title: International Student FAQ - QBridge Program
content: Applicants academically competitive for admission but who do not meet English language proficiency requirements may be considered for the QBridge program, which prepares students linguistically, academically, and culturally for Queen’s undergraduate studies.

title: International Student FAQ - Study Permit
content: International students studying in Canada for more than six months must apply for a study permit after receiving their acceptance letter.

title: International Student FAQ - Study Permit Resources
content: Information on study permits is available on the Study Permit FAQ page and the Queen's University International Study Centre (QUIC) website.

title: International Student FAQ - Immigration Support
content: International applicants may contact Carolina Palmer, International Student Advisor, who is regulated by the College of Canadian Immigration Consultants and available for in-person or Calendly meetings.
